sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.87 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Assume that a finite group$G$has a family of$d$-generator subgroups whose indices have no common divisor. Is it true that$G$can be generated by$d + 1$elements? The answer is positive if$G$is solvable (L.\,G.\,Kov\'acs, H.-S.\,Sim, Indag. Math., { 2} (1991), 229--232). For an arbitrary finite group$G$it is proved that$G$can be generated by$d+2$elements (A.\,Lucchini, Commun. Algebra, { 28}, no.\,4 ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
